figures.caption: Table 2: Overall performance and efficiency comparison of selection approaches for fine-tuning LLaVA-v1.5-7b on the Vision-Flan-186K dataset using a 15% (28K samples) sampling ratio across various multimodal evaluation benchmarks. "Rel" indicates relative performance. The best result is bolded and the runner-up is underlined

Table 8: Comparison of computational cost and performance on LLaVA-665K. Time metrics are reported in H100 GPU-hours. “API Cost” refers to extra expenditures for external LLM services (e.g., GPT-4). Rel. indicates overall relative performance of the full-data fine-tuned model.

Fig. 2: Overview of the VisNec Data Selection Framework. Each sample undergoes two forward passes to compute its VisNec score. Samples with non-positive scores are filtered out, and the remainder are grouped via K-Means clustering, from which the top-r% are selected within each cluster for fine-tuning.

Table 1: Overall performance and efficiency comparison of selection approaches for fine-tuning LLaVA-v1.5-7b on the LLaVA-665K dataset using a 15% (98K samples) sampling ratio across various multimodal evaluation benchmarks. "Rel" indicates relative performance.The best result is bolded and the runner-up is underlined
Fig. 3: Qualitative comparison of Low VisNec (−1.2087) and High VisNec (3.4727) samples, illustrating misaligned, redundant and visual dependent samples.
Fig. 4: (a) Impact of the number of clusters k in K-means. (b) Impact of the sampling ratio ρ.
